{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_686f78c4aa7342bfa4b352ee","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Schema migration system for TaskStore backends.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Migrations are numbered SQL files embedded in the binary at compile time.\n4\t//! Each migration is applied exactly once, in order, and tracked in the\n5\t//! schema_versions table (SQLite) or schema_version key (Redis).\n6\t//!\n7\t//! ## Version checking\n8\t//!\n9\t//! - Binary version = max migration number compiled into the binary\n10\t//! - Store version = max migration applied to the store\n11\t//! - On startup: if store version > binary version, refuse to start with\n12\t//!   MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead — operator must restore from backup or\n13\t//!   upgrade the binary.\n14\t//! - If binary version > store version, apply pending migrations.\n15\t\n16\tuse crate::Result;\n17\tuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\n18\t\n19\t/// A single migration with its version number and SQL content.\n20\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n21\tpub struct Migration {\n22\t    /// Monotonic version number (e.g., 1 for 001_initial.sql)\n23\t    pub version: i64,\n24\t    /// Raw SQL to execute for this migration\n25\t    pub sql: &'static str,\n26\t}\n27\t\n28\t/// Registry of all available migrations compiled into the binary.\n29\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n30\tpub struct MigrationRegistry {\n31\t    /// Ordered map of version -> migration\n32\t    migrations: BTreeMap<i64, Migration>,\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\timpl MigrationRegistry {\n36\t    /// Create a new registry from a list of migrations.\n37\t    ///\n38\t    /// Panics if migration versions are not unique.\n39\t    pub fn new(migrations: &[Migration]) -> Self {\n40\t        let mut map = BTreeMap::new();\n41\t        for m in migrations {\n42\t            if map.insert(m.version, m.clone()).is_some() {\n43\t                panic!(\"duplicate migration version: {}\", m.version);\n44\t            }\n45\t        }\n46\t        Self { migrations: map }\n47\t    }\n48\t\n49\t    /// Get the maximum migration version (the binary's schema version).\n50\t    pub fn max_version(&self) -> i64 {\n51\t        self.migrations.keys().last().copied().unwrap_or(0)\n52\t    }\n53\t\n54\t    /// Get all migrations that need to be applied to reach the target version.\n55\t    ///\n56\t    /// Returns migrations with version > `current_version` in ascending order.\n57\t    pub fn pending_migrations(&self, current_version: i64) -> Vec<&Migration> {\n58\t        self.migrations\n59\t            .values()\n60\t            .filter(|m| m.version > current_version)\n61\t            .collect()\n62\t    }\n63\t\n64\t    /// Validate that the store version is not ahead of the binary version.\n65\t    ///\n66\t    /// Returns an error if store_version > binary_version.\n67\t    pub fn validate_version(&self, store_version: i64) -> Result<()> {\n68\t        let binary_version = self.max_version();\n69\t        if store_version > binary_version {\n70\t            return Err(crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n71\t                store_version,\n72\t                binary_version,\n73\t            });\n74\t        }\n75\t        Ok(())\n76\t    }\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\t/// Build the migration registry from embedded migration files.\n80\t///\n81\t/// This function is generated by the macro that embeds migration files.\n82\t/// For now, we manually construct the registry with the available migrations.\n83\tpub fn build_registry() -> MigrationRegistry {\n84\t    MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n85\t        Migration {\n86\t            version: 1,\n87\t            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/001_initial.sql\"),\n88\t        },\n89\t        Migration {\n90\t            version: 2,\n91\t            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/002_feature_tables.sql\"),\n92\t        },\n93\t        Migration {\n94\t            version: 3,\n95\t            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/003_task_registry_fields.sql\"),\n96\t        },\n97\t    ])\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\t#[cfg(test)]\n101\tmod tests {\n102\t    use super::*;\n103\t\n104\t    #[test]\n105\t    fn test_registry_max_version() {\n106\t        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n107\t            Migration {\n108\t                version: 1,\n109\t                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n110\t            },\n111\t            Migration {\n112\t                version: 2,\n113\t                sql: \"ALTER TABLE foo ADD COLUMN name TEXT;\",\n114\t            },\n115\t        ]);\n116\t\n117\t        assert_eq!(registry.max_version(), 2);\n118\t    }\n119\t\n120\t    #[test]\n121\t    fn test_pending_migrations() {\n122\t        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n123\t            Migration {\n124\t                version: 1,\n125\t                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n126\t            },\n127\t            Migration {\n128\t                version: 2,\n129\t                sql: \"ALTER TABLE foo ADD COLUMN name TEXT;\",\n130\t            },\n131\t            Migration {\n132\t                version: 3,\n133\t                sql: \"CREATE INDEX idx_foo_name ON foo(name);\",\n134\t            },\n135\t        ]);\n136\t\n137\t        // No pending migrations when at max version\n138\t        assert!(registry.pending_migrations(3).is_empty());\n139\t\n140\t        // All migrations pending when at version 0\n141\t        let pending = registry.pending_migrations(0);\n142\t        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 3);\n143\t        assert_eq!(pending[0].version, 1);\n144\t        assert_eq!(pending[1].version, 2);\n145\t        assert_eq!(pending[2].version, 3);\n146\t\n147\t        // Only migrations 2 and 3 pending when at version 1\n148\t        let pending = registry.pending_migrations(1);\n149\t        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 2);\n150\t        assert_eq!(pending[0].version, 2);\n151\t        assert_eq!(pending[1].version, 3);\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    #[test]\n155\t    fn test_validate_version_success() {\n156\t        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[Migration {\n157\t            version: 5,\n158\t            sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n159\t        }]);\n160\t\n161\t        // Store at same version is OK\n162\t        registry.validate_version(5).unwrap();\n163\t\n164\t        // Store behind binary is OK (will apply migrations)\n165\t        registry.validate_version(3).unwrap();\n166\t\n167\t        // Fresh store (version 0) is OK\n168\t        registry.validate_version(0).unwrap();\n169\t    }\n170\t\n171\t    #[test]\n172\t    fn test_validate_version_store_ahead() {\n173\t        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[Migration {\n174\t            version: 3,\n175\t            sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n176\t        }]);\n177\t\n178\t        // Store ahead of binary should fail\n179\t        let result = registry.validate_version(5);\n180\t        assert!(result.is_err());\n181\t        match result.unwrap_err() {\n182\t            crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n183\t                store_version,\n184\t                binary_version,\n185\t            } => {\n186\t                assert_eq!(store_version, 5);\n187\t                assert_eq!(binary_version, 3);\n188\t            }\n189\t            _ => panic!(\"expected SchemaVersionAhead error\"),\n190\t        }\n191\t    }\n192\t\n193\t    #[test]\n194\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_1138c6889bbe49a6bce09a18","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Shard migration cutover state machine.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Implements the node-addition migration flow from plan §4 with explicit state\n4\t//! transitions and a race-window-safe cutover sequence.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! ## Race window analysis (plan §15 OP#1)\n7\t//!\n8\t//! The dangerous window is between \"mark node active\" (routing changes to new-node-only)\n9\t//! and \"delete migrated shard from old node.\" A document written during dual-write that\n10\t//! succeeded on OLD but failed on NEW — and arrived after the last migration page —\n11\t//! would be deleted from OLD without ever reaching NEW.\n12\t//!\n13\t//! ## Solution: quiesce-then-verify cutover\n14\t//!\n15\t//! Instead of the naïve sequence (mark active → stop dual-write → delete old), we use:\n16\t//!\n17\t//! 1. Stop dual-write (no new writes go to either node for affected shards)\n18\t//! 2. Drain: wait for all in-flight writes to both OLD and NEW to complete\n19\t//! 3. Delta migration: re-read affected shards from OLD (catches anything written since\n20\t//!    the last migration page) and write deltas to NEW\n21\t//! 4. Mark node active (routing switches to NEW-only)\n22\t//! 5. Delete migrated shard from OLD\n23\t//!\n24\t//! Step 3 is the key: it closes the race window by ensuring NEW has a complete picture\n25\t//! before we commit the routing change. The cost is one extra pagination pass over each\n26\t//! migrated shard — bounded by the number of docs written during the migration window.\n27\t\n28\tuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\n29\tuse std::fmt;\n30\tuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n31\t\n32\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n33\t\n34\t/// Unique identifier for a shard migration operation.\n35\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n36\tpub struct MigrationId(pub u64);\n37\t\n38\timpl fmt::Display for MigrationId {\n39\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n40\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.0)\n41\t    }\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\t/// Identifier for a physical node in the cluster.\n45\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n46\tpub struct NodeId(pub String);\n47\t\n48\timpl fmt::Display for NodeId {\n49\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n50\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.0)\n51\t    }\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\t/// Identifier for a logical shard.\n55\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n56\tpub struct ShardId(pub u32);\n57\t\n58\timpl fmt::Display for ShardId {\n59\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n60\t        write!(f, \"s{}\", self.0)\n61\t    }\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t/// Per-shard migration state within a node-addition migration.\n65\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n66\tpub enum ShardMigrationState {\n67\t    /// Waiting for background migration to begin.\n68\t    Pending,\n69\t    /// Background pagination is reading docs from source and writing to target.\n70\t    Migrating {\n71\t        docs_copied: u64,\n72\t        pages_remaining: u32,\n73\t    },\n74\t    /// Background migration complete, awaiting cutover.\n75\t    MigrationComplete { docs_copied: u64 },\n76\t    /// Dual-write stopped, in-flight writes draining.\n77\t    Draining {\n78\t        in_flight_count: u32,\n79\t        docs_copied: u64,\n80\t    },\n81\t    /// Delta pass: re-reading source to catch stragglers written during migration.\n82\t    DeltaPass {\n83\t        docs_copied: u64,\n84\t        delta_docs_copied: u64,\n85\t    },\n86\t    /// Node is active for this shard; old replica data deleted.\n87\t    Active,\n88\t    /// Migration failed at this phase.\n89\t    Failed { phase: String, reason: String },\n90\t}\n91\t\n92\timpl fmt::Display for ShardMigrationState {\n93\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n94\t        match self {\n95\t            Self::Pending => write!(f, \"pending\"),\n96\t            Self::Migrating {\n97\t                docs_copied,\n98\t                pages_remaining,\n99\t            } => {\n100\t                write!(\n101\t                    f,\n102\t                    \"migrating({docs_copied} copied, {pages_remaining} pages left)\"\n103\t                )\n104\t            }\n105\t            Self::MigrationComplete { docs_copied } => {\n106\t                write!(f, \"migration_complete({docs_copied} copied)\")\n107\t            }\n108\t            Self::Draining {\n109\t                in_flight_count,\n110\t                docs_copied,\n111\t            } => {\n112\t                write!(\n113\t                    f,\n114\t                    \"draining({in_flight_count} in-flight, {docs_copied} copied)\"\n115\t                )\n116\t            }\n117\t            Self::DeltaPass {\n118\t                docs_copied,\n119\t                delta_docs_copied,\n120\t            } => {\n121\t                write!(f, \"delta_pass({docs_copied} + {delta_docs_copied} copied)\")\n122\t            }\n123\t            Self::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n124\t            Self::Failed { phase, reason } => write!(f, \"failed({phase}: {reason})\"),\n125\t        }\n126\t    }\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/// Overall migration phase for a node addition.\n130\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n131\tpub enum MigrationPhase {\n132\t    /// Computing which shards move to the new node.\n133\t    ComputingAssignments,\n134\t    /// Dual-write active; background migration in progress.\n135\t    DualWriteMigrating,\n136\t    /// Background migration done; beginning cutover.\n137\t    CutoverBegin,\n138\t    /// Stopping dual-write; waiting for in-flight writes to settle.\n139\t    CutoverDraining,\n140\t    /// Re-reading source to catch docs written during migration.\n141\t    CutoverDeltaPass,\n142\t    /// Marking new node active; switching routing.\n143\t    CutoverActivate,\n144\t    /// Deleting migrated shard data from old nodes.\n145\t    CutoverCleanup,\n146\t    /// All shards migrated; migration complete.\n147\t    Complete,\n148\t    /// Migration failed.\n149\t    Failed(String),\n150\t}\n151\t\n152\timpl fmt::Display for MigrationPhase {\n153\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n154\t        match self {\n155\t            Self::ComputingAssignments => write!(f, \"computing_assignments\"),\n156\t            Self::DualWriteMigrating => write!(f, \"dual_write_migrating\"),\n157\t            Self::CutoverBegin => write!(f, \"cutover_begin\"),\n158\t            Self::CutoverDraining => write!(f, \"cutover_draining\"),\n159\t            Self::CutoverDeltaPass => write!(f, \"cutover_delta_pass\"),\n160\t            Self::CutoverActivate => write!(f, \"cutover_activate\"),\n161\t            Self::CutoverCleanup => write!(f, \"cutover_cleanup\"),\n162\t            Self::Complete => write!(f, \"complete\"),\n163\t            Self::Failed(msg) => write!(f, \"failed({msg})\"),\n164\t        }\n165\t    }\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// A single document write targeting a shard during migration.\n169\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n170\tpub struct InFlightWrite {\n171\t    pub doc_id: String,\n172\t    pub shard: ShardId,\n173\t    pub target_nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n174\t    pub completed_nodes: HashSet<NodeId>,\n175\t    pub failed_nodes: HashMap<NodeId, String>,\n176\t    pub submitted_at: Instant,\n177\t}\n178\t\n179\t// Serialize Instant as a placeholder bool (present/absent).\n180\t// Instant is monotonic and not meaningfully serializable across processes;\n181\t// on deserialize, reconstruct as Instant::now().\n182\tmod instant_serde {\n183\t    use serde::{Deserialize, Deserializer, Serialize, Serializer};\n184\t    use std::time::Instant;\n185\t\n186\t    pub fn serialize<S>(instant: &Option<Instant>, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error>\n187\t    where\n188\t        S: Serializer,\n189\t    {\n190\t        instant.is_some().serialize(serializer)\n191\t    }\n192\t\n193\t    pub fn deserialize<'de, D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Option<Instant>, D::Error>\n194\t    where\n195\t        D: Deserializer<'de>,\n196\t    {\n197\t        let present = bool::deserialize(deserializer)?;\n198\t        Ok(if present { Some(Instant::now()) } else { None })\n199\t    }\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\t/// Configuration for migration cutover behavior.\n203\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n204\tpub struct MigrationConfig {\n205\t    /// Maximum time to wait for in-flight writes to drain during cutover.\n206\t    pub drain_timeout: Duration,\n207\t    /// Whether to perform the delta pass (re-read source after stopping dual-write).\n208\t    /// Disabling this saves a pagination pass but opens the race window — only safe\n209\t    /// when anti-entropy is enabled as a safety net.\n210\t    pub skip_delta_pass: bool,\n211\t    /// Whether anti-entropy is enabled — used to determine if skip_delta_pass is safe.\n212\t    pub anti_entropy_enabled: bool,\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\timpl Default for MigrationConfig {\n216\t    fn default() -> Self {\n217\t        Self {\n218\t            drain_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n219\t            skip_delta_pass: false,\n220\t            anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n221\t        }\n222\t    }\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\t/// Error type for migration operations.\n226\t#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\n227\tpub enum MigrationError {\n228\t    #[error(\n229\t        \"anti-entropy is disabled and delta pass is skipped — documents may be lost at cutover\"\n230\t    )]\n231\t    UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy,\n232\t    #[error(\"drain timeout exceeded: {0} in-flight writes still pending\")]\n233\t    DrainTimeout(u32),\n234\t    #[error(\"shard {0} is not in a valid state for this transition (current: {1})\")]\n235\t    InvalidTransition(ShardId, String),\n236\t    #[error(\"migration {0} not found\")]\n237\t    NotFound(MigrationId),\n238\t    #[error(\"delta pass failed for shard {0}: {1}\")]\n239\t    DeltaPassFailed(ShardId, String),\n240\t}\n241\t\n242\t/// Tracks the state of a node-addition migration.\n243\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n244\tpub struct MigrationState {\n245\t    pub id: MigrationId,\n246\t    pub new_node: NodeId,\n247\t    pub replica_group: u32,\n248\t    pub phase: MigrationPhase,\n249\t    pub affected_shards: HashMap<ShardId, ShardMigrationState>,\n250\t    /// Maps shard → old node that currently owns it.\n251\t    pub old_owners: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId>,\n252\t    #[serde(with = \"instant_serde\")]\n253\t    pub started_at: Option<Instant>,\n254\t    #[serde(with = \"instant_serde\")]\n255\t    pub completed_at: Option<Instant>,\n256\t}\n257\t\n258\t/// The migration coordinator manages shard migration state transitions.\n259\tpub struct MigrationCoordinator {\n260\t    config: MigrationConfig,\n261\t    migrations: HashMap<MigrationId, MigrationState>,\n262\t    next_id: u64,\n263\t    /// In-flight writes being tracked for drain during cutover.\n264\t    in_flight: Vec<InFlightWrite>,\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\timpl MigrationCoordinator {\n268\t    pub fn new(config: MigrationConfig) -> Self {\n269\t        Self {\n270\t            config,\n271\t            migrations: HashMap::new(),\n272\t            next_id: 0,\n273\t            in_flight: Vec::new(),\n274\t        }\n275\t    }\n276\t\n277\t    /// Validate migration safety before starting. Returns an error if the configuration\n278\t    /// would allow data loss at the cutover boundary.\n279\t    pub fn validate_safety(&self) -> Result<(), MigrationError> {\n280\t        if self.config.skip_delta_pass && !self.config.anti_entropy_enabled {\n281\t            return Err(MigrationError::UnsafeCutoverNoAntiEntropy);\n282\t        }\n283\t        Ok(())\n284\t    }\n285\t\n286\t    /// Begin a new node-addition migration.\n287\t    pub fn begin_migration(\n288\t        &mut self,\n289\t        new_node: NodeId,\n290\t        replica_group: u32,\n291\t        affected_shards: HashMap<ShardId, NodeId>,\n292\t    ) -> Result<MigrationId, MigrationError> {\n293\t        self.validate_safety()?;\n294\t\n295\t        let id = MigrationId(self.next_id);\n296\t        self.next_id += 1;\n297\t\n298\t        let shard_states: HashMap<ShardId, ShardMigrationState> = affected_shards\n299\t            .keys()\n300\t            .map(|&shard| (shard, ShardMigrationState::Pending))\n301\t            .collect();\n302\t\n303\t        let state = MigrationState {\n304\t            id,\n305\t            new_node,\n306\t            replica_group,\n307\t            phase: MigrationPhase::ComputingAssignments,\n308\t            affected_shards: shard_states,\n309\t            old_owners: affected_shards,\n310\t            started_at: Some(Instant::now()),\n311\t            completed_at: None,\n312\t        };\n313\t\n314\t        self.migrations.insert(id, state);\n315\t        Ok(id)\n316\t    }\n317\t\n318\t    /// Transition to dual-write + background migration phase.\n319\t    pub fn begin_dual_write(&mut self, id: MigrationId) -> Result<(), MigrationError> {\n320\t        let state = self\n321\t            .migrations\n322\t            .get_mut(&id)\n323\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?;\n324\t        state.phase = MigrationPhase::DualWriteMigrating;\n325\t        for shard_state in state.affected_shards.values_mut() {\n326\t            if *shard_state == ShardMigrationState::Pending {\n327\t                *shard_state = ShardMigrationState::Migrating {\n328\t                    docs_copied: 0,\n329\t                    pages_remaining: 0,\n330\t                };\n331\t            }\n332\t        }\n333\t        Ok(())\n334\t    }\n335\t\n336\t    /// Record that a shard's background migration completed.\n337\t    pub fn shard_migration_complete(\n338\t        &mut self,\n339\t        id: MigrationId,\n340\t        shard: ShardId,\n341\t        docs_copied: u64,\n342\t    ) -> Result<(), MigrationError> {\n343\t        let state = self\n344\t            .migrations\n345\t            .get_mut(&id)\n346\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?;\n347\t        let shard_state = state.affected_shards.get_mut(&shard).ok_or_else(|| {\n348\t            MigrationError::InvalidTransition(shard, \"shard not in migration\".into())\n349\t        })?;\n350\t\n351\t        match shard_state {\n352\t            ShardMigrationState::Migrating { .. } => {\n353\t                *shard_state = ShardMigrationState::MigrationComplete { docs_copied };\n354\t            }\n355\t            _ => {\n356\t                return Err(MigrationError::InvalidTransition(\n357\t                    shard,\n358\t                    shard_state.to_string(),\n359\t                ));\n360\t            }\n361\t        }\n362\t\n363\t        // Check if all shards are done migrating\n364\t        let all_complete = state\n365\t            .affected_shards\n366\t            .values()\n367\t            .all(|s| matches!(s, ShardMigrationState::MigrationComplete { .. }));\n368\t\n369\t        if all_complete {\n370\t            state.phase = MigrationPhase::CutoverBegin;\n371\t        }\n372\t\n373\t        Ok(())\n374\t    }\n375\t\n376\t    /// Begin the cutover sequence: stop dual-write and drain in-flight writes.\n377\t    pub fn begin_cutover(&mut self, id: MigrationId) -> Result<MigrationPhase, MigrationError> {\n378\t        let state = self\n379\t            .migrations\n380\t            .get_mut(&id)\n381\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?;\n382\t\n383\t        if !matches!(state.phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverBegin) {\n384\t            return Err(MigrationError::InvalidTransition(\n385\t                ShardId(0),\n386\t                format!(\"expected CutoverBegin, got {}\", state.phase),\n387\t            ));\n388\t        }\n389\t\n390\t        // Transition all shards to Draining\n391\t        let total_in_flight = self.in_flight.len() as u32;\n392\t        for (shard, shard_state) in state.affected_shards.iter_mut() {\n393\t            match shard_state {\n394\t                ShardMigrationState::MigrationComplete { docs_copied } => {\n395\t                    *shard_state = ShardMigrationState::Draining {\n396\t                        in_flight_count: total_in_flight,\n397\t                        docs_copied: *docs_copied,\n398\t                    };\n399\t                }\n400\t                _ => {\n401\t                    return Err(MigrationError::InvalidTransition(\n402\t                        *shard,\n403\t                        shard_state.to_string(),\n404\t                    ));\n405\t                }\n406\t            }\n407\t        }\n408\t\n409\t        state.phase = MigrationPhase::CutoverDraining;\n410\t        Ok(state.phase.clone())\n411\t    }\n412\t\n413\t    /// Register an in-flight write for tracking during drain.\n414\t    pub fn register_in_flight(&mut self, write: InFlightWrite) {\n415\t        self.in_flight.push(write);\n416\t    }\n417\t\n418\t    /// Acknowledge completion of a write to a specific node.\n419\t    pub fn ack_write(&mut self, doc_id: &str, node: &NodeId) {\n420\t        for write in &mut self.in_flight {\n421\t            if write.doc_id == doc_id {\n422\t                write.completed_nodes.insert(node.clone());\n423\t            }\n424\t        }\n425\t    }\n426\t\n427\t    /// Mark a write as failed on a specific node.\n428\t    pub fn fail_write(&mut self, doc_id: &str, node: &NodeId, reason: String) {\n429\t        for write in &mut self.in_flight {\n430\t            if write.doc_id == doc_id {\n431\t                write.failed_nodes.insert(node.clone(), reason.clone());\n432\t            }\n433\t        }\n434\t    }\n435\t\n436\t    /// Check if all in-flight writes have completed (drained).\n437\t    pub fn is_drained(&self) -> bool {\n438\t        self.in_flight\n439\t            .iter()\n440\t            .all(|w| w.completed_nodes.len() + w.failed_nodes.len() == w.target_nodes.len())\n441\t    }\n442\t\n443\t    /// Complete the drain and move to delta pass or activation.\n444\t    pub fn complete_drain(&mut self, id: MigrationId) -> Result<MigrationPhase, MigrationError> {\n445\t        // First check phase exists without holding mutable borrow\n446\t        let phase = self\n447\t            .migrations\n448\t            .get(&id)\n449\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?\n450\t            .phase\n451\t            .clone();\n452\t\n453\t        if !matches!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDraining) {\n454\t            return Err(MigrationError::InvalidTransition(\n455\t                ShardId(0),\n456\t                format!(\"expected CutoverDraining, got {}\", phase),\n457\t            ));\n458\t        }\n459\t\n460\t        // Check drain status\n461\t        if !self.is_drained() {\n462\t            let remaining = self\n463\t                .in_flight\n464\t                .iter()\n465\t                .filter(|w| w.completed_nodes.len() + w.failed_nodes.len() < w.target_nodes.len())\n466\t                .count() as u32;\n467\t            return Err(MigrationError::DrainTimeout(remaining));\n468\t        }\n469\t\n470\t        // Collect docs that need delta pass\n471\t        let needs_delta = self.collect_delta_candidates(id)?;\n472\t        let skip_delta = self.config.skip_delta_pass;\n473\t\n474\t        // Now get mutable borrow to update state\n475\t        let state = self\n476\t            .migrations\n477\t            .get_mut(&id)\n478\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?;\n479\t\n480\t        if skip_delta {\n481\t            // Skip delta pass — safe only if anti-entropy is enabled\n482\t            state.phase = MigrationPhase::CutoverActivate;\n483\t        } else if needs_delta.is_empty() {\n484\t            state.phase = MigrationPhase::CutoverActivate;\n485\t        } else {\n486\t            state.phase = MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass;\n487\t            for (_shard, shard_state) in state.affected_shards.iter_mut() {\n488\t                if let ShardMigrationState::Draining { docs_copied, .. } = shard_state {\n489\t                    *shard_state = ShardMigrationState::DeltaPass {\n490\t                        docs_copied: *docs_copied,\n491\t                        delta_docs_copied: 0,\n492\t                    };\n493\t                }\n494\t            }\n495\t        }\n496\t\n497\t        self.in_flight.clear();\n498\t\n499\t        // If going to activate, do that now (drop mutable borrow first)\n500\t        let next_phase = state.phase.clone();\n501\t        if matches!(next_phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverActivate) {\n502\t            let _ = state;\n503\t            self.activate_shards(id)?;\n504\t            // Return the new phase after activation\n505\t            return Ok(self\n506\t                .migrations\n507\t                .get(&id)\n508\t                .map(|s| s.phase.clone())\n509\t                .unwrap_or(MigrationPhase::CutoverCleanup));\n510\t        }\n511\t\n512\t        Ok(next_phase)\n513\t    }\n514\t\n515\t    /// Identify writes that need the delta pass — those that succeeded on OLD but\n516\t    /// failed (or never reached) NEW.\n517\t    fn collect_delta_candidates(\n518\t        &self,\n519\t        id: MigrationId,\n520\t    ) -> Result<HashMap<ShardId, Vec<String>>, MigrationError> {\n521\t        let state = self\n522\t            .migrations\n523\t            .get(&id)\n524\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?;\n525\t        let mut candidates: HashMap<ShardId, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n526\t\n527\t        for write in &self.in_flight {\n528\t            let old_owner = match state.old_owners.get(&write.shard) {\n529\t                Some(owner) => owner,\n530\t                None => continue,\n531\t            };\n532\t\n533\t            let succeeded_on_old = write.completed_nodes.contains(old_owner);\n534\t            let succeeded_on_new = write.completed_nodes.contains(&state.new_node);\n535\t\n536\t            // Doc is on OLD but not on NEW — delta pass must catch it\n537\t            if succeeded_on_old && !succeeded_on_new {\n538\t                candidates\n539\t                    .entry(write.shard)\n540\t                    .or_default()\n541\t                    .push(write.doc_id.clone());\n542\t            }\n543\t        }\n544\t\n545\t        Ok(candidates)\n546\t    }\n547\t\n548\t    /// Record that the delta pass completed for a shard.\n549\t    pub fn shard_delta_complete(\n550\t        &mut self,\n551\t        id: MigrationId,\n552\t        shard: ShardId,\n553\t        delta_docs: u64,\n554\t    ) -> Result<(), MigrationError> {\n555\t        let state = self\n556\t            .migrations\n557\t            .get_mut(&id)\n558\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?;\n559\t        let shard_state = state.affected_shards.get_mut(&shard).ok_or_else(|| {\n560\t            MigrationError::InvalidTransition(shard, \"shard not in migration\".into())\n561\t        })?;\n562\t\n563\t        match shard_state {\n564\t            ShardMigrationState::DeltaPass { docs_copied, .. } => {\n565\t                *shard_state = ShardMigrationState::MigrationComplete {\n566\t                    docs_copied: *docs_copied + delta_docs,\n567\t                };\n568\t            }\n569\t            _ => {\n570\t                return Err(MigrationError::InvalidTransition(\n571\t                    shard,\n572\t                    shard_state.to_string(),\n573\t                ));\n574\t            }\n575\t        }\n576\t\n577\t        // Check if all shards done with delta\n578\t        let all_complete = state\n579\t            .affected_shards\n580\t            .values()\n581\t            .all(|s| matches!(s, ShardMigrationState::MigrationComplete { .. }));\n582\t\n583\t        if all_complete {\n584\t            state.phase = MigrationPhase::CutoverActivate;\n585\t            self.activate_shards(id)?;\n586\t        }\n587\t\n588\t        Ok(())\n589\t    }\n590\t\n591\t    /// Mark all affected shards as active on the new node.\n592\t    fn activate_shards(&mut self, id: MigrationId) -> Result<(), MigrationError> {\n593\t        let state = self\n594\t            .migrations\n595\t            .get_mut(&id)\n596\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?;\n597\t\n598\t        for shard_state in state.affected_shards.values_mut() {\n599\t            match shard_state {\n600\t                ShardMigrationState::MigrationComplete { .. }\n601\t                | ShardMigrationState::Draining { .. } => {\n602\t                    *shard_state = ShardMigrationState::Active;\n603\t                }\n604\t                _ => {}\n605\t            }\n606\t        }\n607\t\n608\t        if matches!(state.phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverActivate) {\n609\t            state.phase = MigrationPhase::CutoverCleanup;\n610\t        }\n611\t\n612\t        Ok(())\n613\t    }\n614\t\n615\t    /// Complete the migration by deleting migrated shard data from old nodes.\n616\t    pub fn complete_cleanup(&mut self, id: MigrationId) -> Result<(), MigrationError> {\n617\t        let state = self\n618\t            .migrations\n619\t            .get_mut(&id)\n620\t            .ok_or(MigrationError::NotFound(id))?;\n621\t\n622\t        if !matches!(state.phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverCleanup) {\n623\t            return Err(MigrationError::InvalidTransition(\n624\t                ShardId(0),\n625\t                format!(\"expected CutoverCleanup, got {}\", state.phase),\n626\t            ));\n627\t        }\n628\t\n629\t        state.phase = MigrationPhase::Complete;\n630\t        state.completed_at = Some(Instant::now());\n631\t        Ok(())\n632\t    }\n633\t\n634\t    /// Get the current state of a migration.\n635\t    pub fn get_state(&self, id: MigrationId) -> Option<&MigrationState> {\n636\t        self.migrations.get(&id)\n637\t    }\n638\t\n639\t    /// Check if a write should go to both old and new node (dual-write phase).\n640\t    pub fn is_dual_write_active(&self, shard: ShardId) -> bool {\n641\t        self.migrations.values().any(|m| {\n642\t            matches!(m.phase, MigrationPhase::DualWriteMigrating)\n643\t                && matches!(\n644\t                    m.affected_shards.get(&shard),\n645\t                    Some(ShardMigrationState::Migrating { .. }\n654\t}\n655\t\n656\t#[cfg(test)]\n657\tmod tests {\n658\t    use super::*;\n659\t\n660\t    fn node(s: &str) -> NodeId {\n661\t        NodeId(s.to_string())\n662\t    }\n663\t\n664\t    fn     anti_entropy_enabled: false,\n672\t            skip_delta_pass: false,\n673\t            ..Default::default()\n674\t        };\n675\t        let mut coord = MigrationCoordinator::new(config);\n676\t\n677\t        let affected = HashMap::from([(shard(0), node(\"old-0\")), (shard(1), node(\"old-0\"))]);\n678\t\n679\t        let mid = coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, shard(0), 500).unwrap();\n684\t        coord.shard_migration_complete(mid, shard(1), 300).unwrap();\n685\t\n686\t        // Register an in-flight write that succeeded on OLD but not NEW.\n687\t        // The write must be marked as failed on NEW so is_drained() sees\n688\t        // completed + failed == target count.\n689\t        coord.register_in_flight(InFlightWrite {\n690\t            doc_id: \"doc-at-boundary\".into(),\n691\t            shard: shard(0),\n692\t            target_nodes: vec![node(\"old-0\"), node(\"new-0\")],\n693\t            completed_nodes: HashSet::from([node(\"old-0\")]),\n694\t            failed_nodes: HashMap::from([(node(\"new-0\"), \"write failed\".into())]),\n695\t            submitted_at: Instant::now(),\n696\t        });\n697\t\n698\t        // Cutover\n699\t        coord.begin_cutover(mid).unwrap();\n700\t\n701\t        // The drain sees the in-flight write completed (on old, not on new)\n702\t        // Delta pass should be triggered\n703\t        let phase = coord.complete_drain(mid).unwrap();\n704\t        assert_eq!(phase, MigrationPhase::CutoverDeltaPass);\n705\t\n706\t        // Delta pass catches the straggler\n707\t        coord.shard_delta_complete(mid, shard(0), 1).unwrap();\n708\t        // Shard 1 had no );\",\n50\t        )?;\n51\t\n52\t        let current: Option<i64> = conn\n53\t            .query_row(\n54\t                \"SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_versions\",\n55\t                [],\n56\t SQLite Implementation Structure\n\n**File**: `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/task_store/sqlite.rs`\n\n```rust\npub struct SqliteTaskStore {\n    conn: Mutex<Connection>,\n}\n\nimpl SqliteTaskStore {\n    /// Open (or create) the SQLite database at `path`, configure WAL + busy_timeout.\n    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> { ... }\n\n    /// Open an in-memory database (for tests and single-pod dev).\n    pub fn open_in_memory() -> Result<Self> { ... }\n\n    fn configure(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n        conn.execute_batch(\"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL; PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn run_migration(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n        // Creates schema_versions table if needed\n        // Gets current version from schema_versions\n        // Validates store version not ahead of binary version\n        // Applies pending migrations\n    }\n}\n```\n\nKey characteristics:\n- Uses `rusqlite` with `Mutex<Connection>` for thread-safe access\n- WAL mode enabled for better concurrency\n- 5-second busy timeout for lock contention\n- Migration system embedded via `include_str!` macros\n\n### 3. Database Schema\n\n**Migration files**: `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/migrations/`\n\n**001_initial.sql** - Core tables 1-7:\n- `schema_versions` - Migration tracking (version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, applied_at INTEGER)\n- `tasks` - miroir_id (PK), created_at, status, node_tasks (JSON), error, started_at, finished_at, index_uid, task_type, node_errors (JSON)\n- `node_settings_version` - (index_uid, node_id) PK, version, updated_at\n- `aliases` - name (PK), kind, current_uid, target_uids (JSON), version, created_at, history (JSON)\n- `sessions` - session_id (PK), last_write_mtask_id, last_write_at, pinned_group, min_settings_version, ttl\n- `idempotency_cache` - key (PK), body_sha256 (BLOB), miroir_task_id, expires_at\n- `jobs` - id (PK), type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress\n- `leader_lease` - scope (PK), holder, expires_at\n\n**002_feature_tables.sql** - Tables 8-14:\n- `canaries` - id (PK), name, index_uid, interval_s, query_json, assertions_json, enabled, created_at\n- `canary_runs` - (canary_id, ran_at) PK, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json\n- `cdc_cursors` - (sink_name, index_uid) PK, last_event_seq, updated_at\n- `tenant_map` - api_key_hash (BLOB PK), tenant_id, group_id\n- `rollover_policies` - name (PK), write_alias, read_alias, pattern, triggers_json, retention_json, template_json, enabled\n- `search_ui_config` - index_uid (PK), config_json, updated_at\n- `admin_sessions` - session_id (PK), csrf_token, admin_key_hash, created_at, expires_at, revoked, user_agent, source_ip\n- Index: `admin_sessions_expires` on `expires_at`\n\n**003_task_registry_fields.sql** - Task registry extensions:\n- Adds to `tasks`: started_at, finished_at, index_uid, task_type, node_errors\n\n### 4. Store Instantiation\n\n**File**: `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/task_registry.rs`\n\n```rust\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n    Sqlite(Arc<SqliteTaskStore>),\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    Redis(Arc<RedisTaskStore>),\n}\n\nimpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(Path::new(path))?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Sqlite(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            tokio::runtime::Handle::current()\n                .block_on(RedisTaskStore::open(url))\n        })?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Redis(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// In-memory fallback (no persistence).\n    pub fn in_memory() -> Self {\n        Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 5. Configuration\n\n**File**: `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/config.rs`\n\n```rust\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct TaskStoreConfig {\n    /// `sqlite` or `redis`.\n    pub backend: String,\n    /// Path to SQLite database file (sqlite backend).\n    pub path: String,\n    /// Redis URL (redis backend), e.g. `redis://host:6379`.\n    pub url: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for TaskStoreConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            backend: \"sqlite\".into(),\n            path: \"/data/miroir-tasks.db\".into(),\n            url: String::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 6. renew_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str, holder: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool>;\n    fn get_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<Option<LeaderLeaseRow>>;\n\n    // --- Tables 8-14 (feature-flagged): canaries, canary_runs, cdc_cursors, tenant_map, rollover_policies, search_ui_config, admin_sessions ---\n    // ... (methods for each)\n}\n```\n\n### 2. SQLite Implementation Structure\n\n**File**: `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/task_store/sqlite.rs`\n\n```rust\npub struct SqliteTaskStore {\n    conn: Mutex<Connection>,\n}\n\nimpl SqliteTaskStore {\n    /// Open (or create) the SQLite database at `path`, configure WAL + busy_timeout.\n    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> { ... }\n\n    /// Open an in-memory database (for tests and single-pod dev).\n    pub fn open_in_memory() -> Result<Self> { ... }\n\n    fn configure(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n        conn.execute_batch(\"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL; PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn run_migration(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n        // Creates schema_versions table if needed\n        // Gets current version from schema_versions\n        // Validates store version not ahead of binary version\n        // Applies pending migrations\n    }\n}\n```\n\nKey characteristics:\n- Uses `rusqlite` with `Mutex<Connection>` for thread-safe access\n- WAL mode enabled for better concurrency\n- 5-second busy timeout for lock contention\n- Migration system embedded via `include_str!` macros\n\n### 3. Database Schema\n\n**Migration files**: `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/migrations/`\n\n**001_initial.sql** - Core tables 1-7:\n- `schema_versions` - Migration tracking (version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, applied_at INTEGER)\n- `tasks` - miroir_id (PK), created_at, status, node_tasks (JSON), error, started_at, finished_at, index_uid, task_type, node_errors (JSON)\n- `node_settings_version` - (index_uid, node_id) PK, version, updated_at\n- `aliases` - name (PK), kind, current_uid, target_uids (JSON), version, created_at, history (JSON)\n- `sessions` - session_id (PK), last_write_mtask_id, last_write_at, pinned_group, min_settings_version, ttl\n- `idempotency_cache` - key (PK), body_sha256 (BLOB), miroir_task_id, expires_at\n- `jobs` - id (PK), type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress\n- `leader_lease` - scope (PK), holder, expires_at\n\n**002_feature_tables.sql** - Tables 8-14:\n- `canaries` - id (PK), name, index_uid, interval_s, query_json, assertions_json, enabled, created_at\n- `canary_runs` - (canary_id, ran_at) PK, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json\n- `cdc_cursors` - (sink_name, index_uid) PK, last_event_seq, updated_at\n- `tenant_map` - api_key_hash (BLOB PK), tenant_id, group_id\n- `rollover_policies` - name (PK), write_alias, read_alias, pattern, triggers_json, retention_json, template_json, enabled\n- `search_ui_config` - index_uid (PK), config_json, updated_at\n- `admin_sessions` - session_id (PK), csrf_token, admin_key_hash, created_at, expires_at, revoked, user_agent, source_ip\n- Index: `admin_sessions_expires` on `expires_at`\n\n**003_task_registry_fields.sql** - Task registry extensions:\n- Adds to `tasks`: started_at, finished_at, index_uid, task_type, node_errors\n\n### 4. Store Instantiation\n\n**File**: `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/task_registry.rs`\n\n```rust\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n    Sqlite(Arc<SqliteTaskStore>),\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    Redis(Arc<RedisTaskStore>),\n}\n\nimpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(Path::new(path))?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Sqlite(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            tokio::runtime::Handle::current()\n                .block_on(RedisTaskStore::open(url))\n        })?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Redis(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// In-memory fallback (no persistence).\n    pub fn in_memory() -> Self {\n        Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 5. on an async future using a dedicated runtime.\n57\t    /// Always spawns a fresh thread with its own single-threaded runtime to\n58\t    /// avoid \"cannot start a runtime from within a runtime\" panics.\n59\t    fn block_on<F>(&self, future: F) -> F::Output\n60\t    where\n61\t        F: std::future::Future + Send + 'static,\n62\t        F::Output: Send + 'static,\n63\t    {\n64\t        std::thread::scope(|s| {\n65\t            s.spawn(|| {\n66\t                let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n67\t                    .enable_all()\n68\t                    .build()\n69\t                    .expect(\"Failed to create runtime in thread\");\n70\t                rt.block_on(future)\n71\t            })\n72\t            .join()\n73\t            .unwrap_or_else(|_| panic!(\"block_on thread panicked\"))\n74\t        })\n75\t    }\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t/// Redis-backed TaskStore.\n79\t#[derive(Clone)]\n80\tpub struct RedisTaskStore {\n81\t    /// }\n106\t\n107\t    /// Helper to generate key from owned values (for use in async blocks).\n108\t    fn key_owned(prefix: &str, parts: &[String]) -> String {\n109\t        format!(\"{}:{}\", prefix, TaskRow.\n122\t    fn task_from_hash(\n123\t        miroir_id: String,\n124\t        fields: &HashMap<String, Value>,\n125\t    ) -> Result<TaskRow> {\n126\t        let created_at = field: {key}\")))\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t/// Helper: get an i64 field from a Redis hash.\n186\tfn get_field_i64(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Result<i64> {\n187\t    fields\n188\t        .get(key)\n189\t        .and_then(|v| match v {\n190\t            Value::Int(i) => Some(*i),\n191\t            Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes)\n192\t                .ok()\n193\t                .and_then(|s| s.parse::<i64>().ok()),\n194\t            Value::SimpleString(s) => s.parse::<i64>().ok(),\n195\t            _ => None,\n196\t        })\n197\t        .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"missing or invalid field: {key}\")))\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\t/// Helper: convert optional field to Option<String>.\n201\tfn opt_field(fields: })\n208\t}\n209\t\n210\t/// Helper: convert optional field to Option<i64>.\n211\tfn opt_field_i64(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Option<i64> {\n212\t    fields.get(key).and_then(|v| match v {\n213\t        Value::Int(i) => Some(*i),\n214\t        Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes)\n215\t            .ok()\n216\t            .and_then(|s| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n232\t\n233\timpl TaskStore for RedisTaskStore {\n234\t    fn migrate(&self) -> Result<()> {\n235\t        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n236\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n237\t        let version_key = format!(\"{}:schema_version\", key_prefix);\n238\t        self.block_on(async move self.key(&[\"tasks\", \"_index\"]);\n398\t        let status_filter = filter.status.clone();\n399\t        let index_uid_filter = filter.index_uid.clone();\n400\t        let task_type_filter =       }\n427\t                }\n428\t                if let Some(ref index_uid) = index_uid_filter {\n429\t                    if task.index_uid.as_ref() != Some(index_uid) {\n430\t }\n452\t            }\n453\t            if let Some(limit) = limit {\n454\t                tasks.truncate(limit);\n455\t            }\n456\t\n457\t            Ok(tasks)\n458\t        })\n459\t    }\n460\t\n461\t    fn prune_tasks(&self, cutoff_ms: i64, batch_size: u32) -> Result<usize> {\n462\t        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n463\t        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n464\t        let index_key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", \"_index\"]);\n465\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n466\t\n467\t        self.block_on(async move {\n468\t            let mut conn = pool.pipeline_query(&mut p).await?;\n483\t\n484\t                if let (Some(created_at_str), Some(status)) = result {\n485\t                    let created_at: i64 = created_at_str.parse()\n486\t                        .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid created_at: {e}\")))?;\n487\t                    if created_at < cutoff_ms && terminal_statuses.contains(&status.as_str()) Redis-backed TaskStore implementation (plan §4 \"Redis mode (HA)\").\n//!\n//! This module implements the TaskStore trait using Redis as the backend.\n//! Each SQLite table is mapped to a Redis keyspace as specified in plan §4.\n\nuse crate::task_store::*;\nuse crate::Result;\nuse crate::MiroirError;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::SystemTime;\nuse tokio::sync::Mutex;\n\nuse ::redis::aio::ConnectionManager;\nuse ::redis::{\n    pipe, AsyncCommands, Client, ExistenceCheck, FromRedisValue, Pipeline, SetExpiry,\n    SetOptions, Value,\n};\nuse futures_util::StreamExt;\nuse ::redis::AsyncIter;\n\n\n/// Redis connection pool wrapper.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct RedisPool {\n    /// Connection manager for async operations (shared across clones)\n    manager: Arc<Mutex<ConnectionManager>>,\n}\n\nimpl RedisPool {\n    /// Create a new Redis pool from a connection URL.\n    pub async fn new(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let client = Client::open(url).map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n        let conn = client\n            .get_connection_manager()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            manager: Arc::new(Mutex::new(conn)),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a pipeline and return its query result.\n    pub async fn pipeline_query<R>(&self, pipe: &mut Pipeline) -> Result<R>\n    where\n        R: FromRedisValue,\n    {\n        let mut conn = self.manager.lock().await;\n        pipe.query_async(&mut *conn)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n\n    /// Block on an async future using a dedicated runtime.\n    /// Always spawns a fresh thread with its own single-threaded runtime to\n    /// avoid \"cannot start a runtime from within a runtime\" panics.\n    fn block_on<F>(&self, future: F) -> F::Output\n    where\n        F: std::future::Future + Send + 'static,\n        F::Output: Send + 'static,\n    {\n        std::thread::scope(|s| {\n            s.spawn(|| {\n                let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n                    .enable_all()\n                    .build()\n                    .expect(\"Failed to create runtime in thread\");\n                rt.block_on(future)\n            })\n            .join()\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| panic!(\"block_on thread panicked\"))\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Redis-backed TaskStore.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct RedisTaskStore {\n    /// Redis connection pool\n    pool: RedisPool,\n    /// Key prefix for all Miroir keys\n    key_prefix: String,\n}\n\nimpl RedisTaskStore {\n    /// Open a Redis task store from a connection URL.\n    pub async fn open(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let pool = RedisPool::new(url).await?;\n        Ok(Self {\n            pool,\n            key_prefix: \"miroir\".into(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Return the key prefix used by this store.\n    pub fn key_prefix(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.key_prefix\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a fully-qualified Redis key.\n    fn key(&self, parts: &[&str]) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}:{}\", self.key_prefix, parts.join(\":\"))\n    }\n\n    /// Helper to generate key from owned values (for use in async blocks).\n    fn key_owned(prefix: &str, parts: &[String]) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}:{}\", prefix, parts.join(\":\"))\n    }\n\n    /// Helper: run an async future using the dedicated runtime.\n    fn block_on<F>(&self, future: F) -> F::Output\n    where\n        F: std::future::Future + Send + 'static,\n        F::Output: Send + 'static,\n    {\n        self.pool.block_on(future)\n    }\n\n    /// Helper: parse a hash row into a TaskRow.\n    fn task_from_hash(\n        miroir_id: String,\n        fields: &HashMap<String, Value>,\n    ) -> Result<TaskRow> {\n        let created_at = get_field_i64(fields, \"created_at\")?;\n        let status = get_field_string(fields, \"status\")?;\n        let node_tasks_json = get_field_string(fields, \"node_tasks\")?;\n        let node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64> = serde_json::from_str(&node_tasks_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid node_tasks JSON: {e}\")))?;\n        let error = opt_field(fields, \"error\");\n        let started_at = opt_field_i64(fields, \"started_at\");\n        let finished_at = opt_field_i64(fields, \"finished_at\");\n        let index_uid = opt_field(fields, \"index_uid\");\n        let task_type = opt_field(fields, \"task_type\");\n        let node_errors_json = opt_field(fields, \"node_errors\").unwrap_or_else(|| \"{}\".to_string());\n        let node_errors: HashMap<String, String> = serde_json::from_str(&node_errors_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid node_errors JSON: {e}\")))?;\n\n        Ok(TaskRow {\n            miroir_id,\n            created_at,\n            status,\n            node_tasks,\n            error,\n            started_at,\n            finished_at,\n            index_uid,\n            task_type,\n            node_errors,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Helper: parse canary hash row.\n    fn canary_from_hash(\n        id: String,\n        fields: &HashMap<String, Value>,\n    ) -> Result<CanaryRow> {\n        Ok(CanaryRow {\n            id,\n            name: get_field_string(fields, \"name\")?,\n            index_uid: get_field_string(fields, \"index_uid\")?,\n            interval_s: get_field_i64(fields, \"interval_s\")?,\n            query_json: get_field_string(fields, \"query_json\")?,\n            assertions_json: get_field_string(fields, \"assertions_json\")?,\n            enabled: get_field_i64(fields, \"enabled\")? != 0,\n            created_at: get_field_i64(fields, \"created_at\")?,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Helper: get a string field from a Redis hash.\nfn get_field_string(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Result<String> {\n    fields\n        .get(key)\n        .and_then(|v| match v {\n            Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes).ok().map(String::from),\n            Value::Int(i) => Some(i.to_string()),\n            Value::SimpleString(s) => Some(s.clone()),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n        .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"missing field: {key}\")))\n}\n\n/// Helper: get an i64 field from a Redis hash.\nfn get_field_i64(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Result<i64> {\n    fields\n        .get(key)\n        .and_then(|v| match v {\n            Value::Int(i) => Some(*i),\n            Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes)\n                .ok()\n                .and_then(|s| s.parse::<i64>().ok()),\n            Value::SimpleString(s) => s.parse::<i64>().ok(),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n        .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"missing or invalid field: {key}\")))\n}\n\n/// Helper: convert optional field to Option<String>.\nfn opt_field(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    fields.get(key).and_then(|v| match v {\n        Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes).ok().map(String::from),\n        Value::Int(i) => Some(i.to_string()),\n        Value::SimpleString(s) => Some(s.clone()),\n        _ => None,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Helper: convert optional field to Option<i64>.\nfn opt_field_i64(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Option<i64> {\n    fields.get(key).and_then(|v| match v {\n        Value::Int(i) => Some(*i),\n        Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes)\n            .ok()\n            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<i64>().ok()),\n        Value::SimpleString(s) => s.parse::<i64>().ok(),\n        _ => None,\n    })\n}\n\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// TaskStore trait implementation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimpl TaskStore for RedisTaskStore {\n    fn migrate(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let version_key = format!(\"{}:schema_version\", key_prefix);\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let current: Option<i64> = conn.get(&version_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let binary_version = crate::schema_migrations::build_registry()\n                .max_version();\n\n            // Validate that store version is not ahead of binary\n            if let Some(v) = current {\n                if v > binary_version {\n                    return Err(MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n                        store_version: v,\n                        binary_version,\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n\n            // If this is a fresh store, record our version\n            if current.is_none() {\n                let _: () = conn.set(&version_key, binary_version).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 1: tasks ---\n\n    fn insert_task(&self, task: &NewTask) -> Result<()> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let task = task.clone();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:tasks:{}\", key_prefix, task.miroir_id);\n        let index_key = format!(\"{}:tasks:_index\", key_prefix);\n        let created_at_str = task.created_at.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let node_tasks_json = serde_json::to_string(&task.node_tasks)?;\n            let node_errors_json = serde_json::to_string(&task.node_errors)?;\n\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.hset_multiple(\n                &key,\n                &[\n                    (\"miroir_id\", task.miroir_id.as_str()),\n                    (\"created_at\", created_at_str.as_str()),\n                    (\"status\", task.status.as_str()),\n                    (\"node_tasks\", node_tasks_json.as_str()),\n                    (\"node_errors\", node_errors_json.as_str()),\n                ],\n            );\n            if let Some(ref error) = task.error {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"error\", error);\n            }\n            if let Some(started_at) = task.started_at {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"started_at\", started_at);\n            }\n            if let Some(finished_at) = task.finished_at {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"finished_at\", finished_at);\n            }\n            if let Some(ref index_uid) = task.index_uid {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"index_uid\", index_uid);\n            }\n            if let Some(ref task_type) = task.task_type {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"task_type\", task_type);\n            }\n            pipe.sadd(&index_key, &task.miroir_id);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<TaskRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", miroir_id]);\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if fields.is_empty() {\n                Ok(None)\n            } else {\n                Ok(Some(Self::task_from_hash(miroir_id, &fields)?))\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_task_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", miroir_id]);\n        let status = status.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let exists: bool = conn.hexists(&key, \"miroir_id\").await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if !exists {\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n\n            let _: () = conn.hset(&key, \"status\", &status).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            Ok(true)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_node_task(&self, miroir_id: &str, node_id: &str, task_uid: u64) -> Result<bool> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", miroir_id]);\n        let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let node_tasks_json: Option<String> = conn.hget(&key, \"node_tasks\").await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let Some(json) = node_tasks_json else {\n                return Ok(false);\n            };\n\n            let mut map: HashMap<String, u64> = serde_json::from_str(&json)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid node_tasks JSON: {e}\")))?;\n            map.insert(node_id, task_uid);\n            let updated = serde_json::to_string(&map)?;\n\n            let _: () = conn.hset(&key, \"node_tasks\", &updated).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            Ok(true)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn set_task_error(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", miroir_id]);\n        let error = error.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let exists: bool = conn.hexists(&key, \"miroir_id\").await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if !exists {\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n\n            let _: () = conn.hset(&key, \"error\", &error).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            Ok(true)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn list_tasks(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<TaskRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let index_key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", \"_index\"]);\n        let status_filter = filter.status.clone();\n        let index_uid_filter = filter.index_uid.clone();\n        let task_type_filter = filter.task_type.clone();\n        let limit = filter.limit;\n        let offset = filter.offset;\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let all_ids: Vec<String> = conn.smembers(&index_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n            for miroir_id in all_ids {\n                let key = format!(\"{}:tasks:{}\", key_prefix, miroir_id);\n                let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n                if fields.is_empty() {\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                let task = Self::task_from_hash(miroir_id, &fields)?;\n\n                // Apply filters\n                if let Some(ref status) = status_filter {\n                    if &task.status != status {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n                if let Some(ref index_uid) = index_uid_filter {\n                    if task.index_uid.as_ref() != Some(index_uid) {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n                if let Some(ref task_type) = task_type_filter {\n                    if task.task_type.as_ref() != Some(task_type) {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                tasks.push(task);\n            }\n\n            // Sort by created_at DESC\n            tasks.sort_by(|a, b| b.created_at.cmp(&a.created_at));\n\n            // Apply pagination\n            if let Some(offset) = offset {\n                if offset < tasks.len() {\n                    tasks.drain(0..offset);\n                } else {\n                    tasks.clear();\n                }\n            }\n            if let Some(limit) = limit {\n                tasks.truncate(limit);\n            }\n\n            Ok(tasks)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn prune_tasks(&self, cutoff_ms: i64, batch_size: u32) -> Result<usize> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let index_key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", \"_index\"]);\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let all_ids: Vec<String> = conn.smembers(&index_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let terminal_statuses = [\"succeeded\", \"failed\", \"canceled\"];\n            let mut to_delete = Vec::new();\n\n            for miroir_id in all_ids.into_iter().take(batch_size as usize) {\n                let key = format!(\"{}:tasks:{}\", key_prefix, miroir_id);\n\n                // Use a pipeline to get both fields atomically\n                let mut p = pipe();\n                p.hget(&key, \"created_at\");\n                p.hget(&key, \"status\");\n                let result: (Option<String>, Option<String>) = pool.pipeline_query(&mut p).await?;\n\n                if let (Some(created_at_str), Some(status)) = result {\n                    let created_at: i64 = created_at_str.parse()\n                        .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid created_at: {e}\")))?;\n                    if created_at < cutoff_ms && terminal_statuses.contains(&status.as_str()) {\n                        to_delete.push(miroir_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            if to_delete.is_empty() {\n                return Ok(0);\n            }\n\n            // Delete tasks and remove from index\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            for miroir_id in &to_delete {\n                let key = format!(\"{}:tasks:{}\", key_prefix, );\n620\t            if let Some(ref current_uid) = current_uid {\n621\t                pipe.hset(&key, \"current_uid\", current_uid);\n622\t            }\n623\t            if has_target_uids {\n624\t self.pool.manager.clone();\n673\t        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n674\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n675\t        let name = name.to_string();\n676\t        let new_uid =            let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n682\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n683\t\n684\t            if fields.is_empty() {\n685\t                return Ok(false);\n686\t            }\n687\t\n688\t            let old_uid = opt_field(&fields, \"current_uid\").unwrap_or_default();\n689\t            let old_version = get_field_i64(&fields, \"version\")?;\n690\t            let history_json = get_field_string(&fields, \"history\")?;\n691\t            let mut history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)\n692\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid history JSON: {e}\")))?;\n693\t\n694\t            if !old_uid.is_empty() {\n695\t                history.push(AliasHistoryEntry {\n696\t                    uid: old_uid,\n697\t                    flipped_at: now_ms(),\n698\t                });\n699\t            }\n700\t            while history.len() > history_retention {\n701\t                history.remove(0);\n702\t            }\n703\t\n704\t            let new_history_json = serde_json::to_string(&history)?;\n705\t            let new_version_str = (old_version + 1).to_string();\n706\t\n707\t            // Use pipeline_query for the atomic update\n708\t            let mut pipe = pipe();\n709\t            pipe.hset(&key, \"current_uid\", &new_uid);\n710\t            pipe.hset(&key, \"version\", &new_version_str);\n711\t            pipe.hset(&key, \"history\", &new_history_json);\n712\t            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n713\t\n714\t            Ok(true)\n715\t        })\n716\t    }\n717\t\n718\t    fn delete_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n719\t        let pool Ok(count)\n        })\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 2: node_settings_version ---\n\n    fn upsert_node_settings_version(\n        &self,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n ---\n\n    fn create_alias(&self, alias: &NewAlias) -> Result<()> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let name = alias.name.clone();\n        let kind = alias.kind.clone();\n        let target_uids_json = alias\n            .target_uids\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|uids| serde_json::to_string(uids))\n            .transpose()?\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n        let history_json = serde_json::to_string(&alias.history)?;\n        let version_str = alias.version.to_string();\n        let created_at_str = alias.created_at.to_string();\n        let current_uid = alias.current_uid.clone();\n        let has_target_uids = alias.target_uids.is_some();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:aliases:{}\", key_prefix, name);\n        let index_key = format!(\"{}:aliases:_index\", key_prefix);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.hset_multiple(\n                &key,\n                &[\n                    (\"name\", name.as_str()),\n                    (\"kind\", kind.as_str()),\n                    (\"version\", version_str.as_str()),\n                    (\"created_at\", created_at_str.as_str()),\n                    (\"history\", history_json.as_str()),\n                ],\n            );\n            if let Some(ref current_uid) = current_uid {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"current_uid\", current_uid);\n            }\n            if has_target_uids {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"target_uids\", &target_uids_json);\n            }\n            pipe.sadd(&index_key, &name);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<Option<AliasRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let name = name.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:aliases:{}\", key_prefix, name);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if fields.is_empty() {\n                Ok(None)\n            } else {\n                let history_json = get_field_string(&fields, \"history\")?;\n                let history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid history JSON: {e}\")))?;\n\n                let target_uids = opt_field(&fields, \"target_uids\")\n                    .map(|json| {\n           .transpose()?;\n\n                Ok(Some(AliasRow {\n                    name: name.clone(),\n                    kind: get_field_string(&fields, \"kind\")?,\n usize) -> Result<bool> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let name = name.to_string();\n        let new_uid = new_uid.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:aliases:{}\", key_prefix, name);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if fields.is_empty() {\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n\n            let old_uid = opt_field(&fields, \"current_uid\").unwrap_or_default();\n            let old_version = get_field_i64(&fields, \"version\")?;\n            let history_json = get_field_string(&fields, \"history\")?;\n            let mut history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid history JSON: {e}\")))?;\n\n            if !old_uid.is_empty() {\n                history.push(AliasHistoryEntry {\n                    uid: old_uid,\n                    flipped_at: now_ms(),\n                });\n            }\n            while history.len() > history_retention {\n                history.remove(0);\n            }\n\n            let new_history_json = serde_json::to_string(&history)?;\n            let new_version_str = (old_version + 1).to_string();\n\n            // Use pipeline_query for the atomic update\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.hset(&key, \"current_uid\", &new_uid);\n            pipe.hset(&key, \"version\", &new_version_str);\n            pipe.hset(&key, \"history\", &new_history_json);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n\n            Ok(true)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn delete_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let name = name.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:aliases:{}\", key_prefix, name);\n        let index_key =   fn upsert_session(&self, session: &SessionRow) -> Result<()> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let session = session.clone();\n Vec::new();\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Use the _index set for O(cardinality) iteration (no SCAN).\n            let index_key = format!(\"{}:jobs:_index\",
